title: Fix bcrypt verify that still uses == for comparison
prompt:
This auth module hashes passwords correctly with `Bcrypt.hash_pwd_salt/1`, but verifies them by hashing the input and comparing with `==`:

```elixir
def verify(email, password) do
  user = Repo.get_by(User, email: email)
  if user && Bcrypt.hash_pwd_salt(password) == user.password_hash do
    {:ok, user}
  else
    {:error, :invalid}
  end
end
```

This is doubly broken: (1) bcrypt hashes are not deterministic — hashing the same password twice produces different outputs, so `==` will NEVER match, (2) `==` is variable-time. Rewrite to use `Bcrypt.verify_pass/2` which internally uses secure_compare, and `Bcrypt.no_user_verify/0` for the user-not-found branch (preserves timing consistency). Write the fix to `lib/my_app/auth.ex`.
tier_rationale: Medium — Claude often assumes bcrypt hashes are deterministic, missing both the correctness bug and the timing leak.
